author: s1na
content:
To summarise the conversation around the minimum history retention required for network stability, a few options came up during the call. They range from 2 weeks to 1 year and each come with a rationale. We also discussed the fact that finalization should play a role into this. If the network doesn't finalize for a while these bounds should move.
- Various limits proposed around the **2 week mark**: weak subjectivity checkpoint (~3500 epochs), blobs retention period (18 days)
- **1 month** came up with the idea to have weak subjectivity checkpoint plus extra buffer (e.g. 8192 epochs for a nice round number)
- **~4-5 months** which coincicdes with the **CL block retention policy**. They've had this for years. Every CL is required to retain 33,024 epochs, regardless of finalization status.
- **1 year** which was stated first in EIP-4444

author: s1na
content: Please let me know if I missed any option that was brought up in the chat

author: s1na
content:
A few points on the CL<>EL interaction that we discussed with <@654267572107083777>:
- Some CLs persist EL payloads and some assume that the EL has them
- CLs don't "need" the payload beyond the weak subjectivity point because that's the oldest point to which they can safely reorg
- The 33,024 epochs of CL block retention doesn't then technically impose a requirement on EL to also retain blocks for that long. Picking that would be just a nice-to-have.
- The thought did come up: what if we have to reorg to the weak subjectivity point or close to it. If we choose a 2 week history retention, at that point the EL will have very little history left, in fact it might not even be enough to execute the BLOCKHASH opcode.
- Which means some buffer over the WSP is needed. One idea is 2x WSP so that even if you reorg back then there is same amount of history available in the database.
